International football competition

The **2005–06 UEFA Cup**, the 35th edition of the [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_League "UEFA Europa League"), was won by [Sevilla](/wiki/Sevilla_FC "Sevilla FC"), beating [Middlesbrough](/wiki/Middlesbrough_F.C. "Middlesbrough F.C.") in the [final](/wiki/2006_UEFA_Cup_final "2006 UEFA Cup final"). It was the first victory for Sevilla in a European competition, and the first appearance by Middlesbrough in a European final. The final took place at [Philips Stadion](/wiki/Philips_Stadion "Philips Stadion"), in [Eindhoven](/wiki/Eindhoven "Eindhoven"), Netherlands. The match was refereed by [Herbert Fandel](/wiki/Herbert_Fandel "Herbert Fandel"). Middlesbrough sealed their place in the final on the back of two dramatic comebacks. In the quarter-finals they beat [FC Basel](/wiki/FC_Basel "FC Basel") of Switzerland 4–3 on aggregate (after losing the first leg 2–0 and being 1–0 down in the second leg, they scored 4 goals), this put them into the semi-final to face [Steaua București](/wiki/FCSB "FCSB"). The first leg finished 1–0 to Steaua, and the second leg (at the [Riverside Stadium](/wiki/Riverside_Stadium "Riverside Stadium") again) finished 4–2 (after being 2–0 down). Sevilla went on to defend the trophy the following year.

[CSKA Moscow](/wiki/PFC_CSKA_Moscow "PFC CSKA Moscow") were the defending champions, but were eliminated in the group stage.

## Association team allocation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2005%E2%80%9306_UEFA_Cup&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Association team allocation")]

113 teams qualified directly for the 2005–06 UEFA Cup from 52 UEFA associations. An additional three teams qualified via the UEFA Fair Play league, while 27 further teams qualified at various stages from the [UEFA Intertoto Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Intertoto_Cup "UEFA Intertoto Cup") and the [UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League").

Below is the final qualification scheme for the 2005–06 UEFA Cup:

* Associations 1–6 each have 3 teams qualify, with the exception of England (2) who have 2 teams
* Associations 7–8 each have 4 teams qualify
* Associations 9–15 each have 2 teams qualify
* Associations 16–20 each have 3 teams qualify
* Associations 21–49 each have 2 teams qualify, with the exception of Liechtenstein (43) who have 1 team
* Associations 50–52 each have 1 team qualify

plus

* Winner of previous years' tournament
* 3 UEFA Fair Play entries
* 3 winners of the [2005 UEFA Intertoto Cup](/wiki/2005_UEFA_Intertoto_Cup "2005 UEFA Intertoto Cup")
* 16 losers from the [UEFA Champions League third qualifying round](/wiki/2005%E2%80%9306_UEFA_Champions_League_qualifying_rounds#Third_qualifying_round "2005–06 UEFA Champions League qualifying rounds")
* 8 third-placed teams from the [UEFA Champions League group stage](/wiki/2005%E2%80%9306_UEFA_Champions_League_group_stage "2005–06 UEFA Champions League group stage")

### Distribution

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2005%E2%80%9306_UEFA_Cup&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Distribution")]

Each association enters a certain number of teams to the UEFA Cup based on its [league coefficient](/wiki/UEFA_coefficients "UEFA coefficients"). Through domestic competitions (national championships and cups and league cups in certain countries) an association may qualify up to four teams. The following amendments were made to the 2005–06 qualification scheme:

* The defending UEFA Cup holder CSKA Moscow already qualified for the 2nd qualifying round, which means that the first UEFA Cup entrant of the 25th and 26th associations on the ranking list (Hungary and Romania) will gain direct access to the second qualifying round of the UEFA Cup.
* Because [Liverpool](/wiki/Liverpool_F.C. "Liverpool F.C.") entered the first qualifying round of the Champions League as title holders, the first UEFA Cup entrant of 11th and 12th associations on the ranking list (Scotland and Belgium) will gain direct access to the first round of the UEFA Cup.
* In Kazakhstan, only [Kairat](/wiki/FC_Kairat "FC Kairat") obtained a UEFA licence for the 2005–06 season. The other teams from Kazakhstan were not allowed to compete. Therefore, the first UEFA Cup entrant of the 27th and 28th countries on the ranking list (Slovakia and Slovenia) will gain direct access to the second qualifying round of the UEFA Cup.
* In [Serbia and Montenegro](/wiki/Serbia_and_Montenegro "Serbia and Montenegro"), the cup winner [Železnik](/wiki/FK_%C5%BDeleznik "FK Železnik") was replaced by [OFK Beograd](/wiki/OFK_Beograd "OFK Beograd") because they did not obtain a UEFA licence.
* In [Bosnia and Herzegovina](/wiki/Bosnia_and_Herzegovina "Bosnia and Herzegovina"), the cup winner [Sarajevo](/wiki/FK_Sarajevo "FK Sarajevo") was replaced by [Široki Brijeg](/wiki/NK_%C5%A0iroki_Brijeg "NK Široki Brijeg"), and 2nd in the league [Zeljeznicar](/wiki/FK_%C5%BDeljezni%C4%8Dar_Sarajevo "FK Željezničar Sarajevo") was replaced by [Žepče](/wiki/NK_%C5%BDep%C4%8De "NK Žepče"), because they did not obtain a UEFA licence.

Notes

1. **[^](#ref_Note_SCG_1)**

   **Serbia and Montenegro (SCG)**: [2004–05 Serbia and Montenegro Cup](/wiki/2004%E2%80%9305_Serbia_and_Montenegro_Cup "2004–05 Serbia and Montenegro Cup") winners [Železnik](/wiki/FK_%C5%BDeleznik "FK Železnik") failed to obtain UEFA licence and was replaced by league 4th-placed club [OFK Beograd](/wiki/OFK_Beograd "OFK Beograd"), as Cup runners-up [Red Star Belgrade](/wiki/Red_Star_Belgrade "Red Star Belgrade") already qualified to UEFA Cup through league position.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
2. **[^](#ref_Note_BIH_1)**

   **Bosnia and Herzegovina (BIH)**: [2004–05 Bosnia and Herzegovina Football Cup](/wiki/2004%E2%80%9305_Bosnia_and_Herzegovina_Football_Cup "2004–05 Bosnia and Herzegovina Football Cup") winners [Sarajevo](/wiki/FK_Sarajevo "FK Sarajevo") and league runners-up [Željezničar](/wiki/FK_%C5%BDeljezni%C4%8Dar_Sarajevo "FK Željezničar Sarajevo") failed to obtain UEFA licences and were replaced by two highest-placed teams that obtained them — [Široki Brijeg](/wiki/NK_%C5%A0iroki_Brijeg "NK Široki Brijeg") (Cup runners-up and league 3rd-placed team) and [Žepče](/wiki/NK_%C5%BDep%C4%8De "NK Žepče") (12th-placed team).[[3]](#cite_note-3)
3. **[^](#ref_Note_KAZ_1)**

   **Kazakhstan (KAZ)**: [2004 Kazakhstan Cup](/w/index.php?title=2004_Kazakhstan_Cup&action=edit&redlink=1 "2004 Kazakhstan Cup (page does not exist)") winners [Taraz](/wiki/FC_Taraz "FC Taraz") and league runners-up [Irtysh](/wiki/FC_Irtysh_Pavlodar "FC Irtysh Pavlodar") failed to obtain UEFA licences. Since no other team was able to obtain a licence as well, Kazakhstan was not represented in UEFA Cup.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

 [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b2/Purple_pog.svg/20px-Purple_pog.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Purple_pog.svg) Purple: Group E; [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c0/Pink_pog.svg/20px-Pink_pog.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Pink_pog.svg) Pink: Group F; [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d7/Blue_pog.svg/20px-Blue_pog.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Blue_pog.svg) Blue: Group G; [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e0/Orange_pog.svg/20px-Orange_pog.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Orange_pog.svg) Orange: Group H.

Based on paragraph 4.06 in the UEFA regulations for the current season, tiebreakers, if necessary, are applied in the following order:[[6]](#cite_note-7)

1. Cumulative goal difference in group matches
2. Total goals scored in group matches
3. Away goals scored in group matches
4. Higher number of [UEFA coefficient](/wiki/UEFA_coefficients "UEFA coefficients") points accumulated by the club in question, as well as its association, over the previous five seasons (see paragraph 6.03 of the UEFA regulations)
